user_query:
We want a complete, household-ready emergency plan for our family in Austin, TX. Please read these files under input/:
- input/household.json
- input/home_layout.md
- input/local_risks.txt

Family members: Nora (mom, 36), Luis (dad, 38), Eli (7), and Abuela Rosa (68). Rosa uses a cane and takes insulin that must be refrigerated. We also have one indoor cat, Luna. Home: two-story townhouse, bedrooms upstairs. Local risks include flash floods, tornado watches/severe thunderstorms, wildfire smoke days, occasional ice storms, heat waves, and multi-hour power outages.

Using what you learn from those files, create the following deliverables under output/ (do not leave any value as "TBD"; if something is uncertain, make a reasonable assumption and record it in assumptions.md). Keep everything tailored to our situation and risks, and use US formats for phone numbers.

1) output/plan/fire_escape.md — Room-by-room fire escape plan with two exits per bedroom, where to store escape ladder(s) if needed, our primary outdoor meeting point, smoke detector placement/maintenance, and a twice-a-year drill plan (include a nighttime drill note).

2) output/plan/communication_plan.json — Must include: out_of_area_contact { name, phone }, meeting_points { home, neighborhood, out_of_area }, communication_methods (ordered list), and wallet_cards_file pointing to output/cards/emergency_cards.csv.

3) output/cards/emergency_cards.csv — One row per family member (at least 4), with headers: name,role,phone_primary,phone_secondary,out_of_area_contact_name,out_of_area_contact_phone,meeting_point_home,meeting_point_neighborhood,meeting_point_out_of_area. Use realistic placeholders where needed, but not "TBD".

4) output/plan/document_kit_checklist.md — Checklist for physical and encrypted-digital copies (IDs, insurance, medical records, prescriptions, bank/contact info, pet records), and recommended emergency cash.

5) output/plan/go_bags.csv — A detailed, per-person 72-hour kit list with columns: owner,category,item,qty,notes. Include medication handling (Rosa/insulin), child comfort item (Eli), pet supplies (Luna), smoke protection (N95s), light/power, water/food, shelter, tools, sanitation, and budget-conscious options. Ensure at least one row for each: Nora, Luis, Eli, Rosa.

6) output/plan/evacuation_routes.json — Three ways out of our neighborhood (primary/alternate/backup) with plain-English descriptions, plus at least two destination options (e.g., friend/relative in another town and a public shelter). Also include an "avoid" list (e.g., low-water crossings during floods) and a quick vehicle kit list.

7) output/plan/utility_shutoffs.md — Where and how to shut off gas, water, and electricity, what tools to keep, when to shut each off, and clear labeling instructions for the household.

8) output/plan/special_needs.md — Step-by-step assistance plan for Rosa (mobility and insulin refrigeration in outages: cooler/ice packs, power outage plan), stair-evac approach, and who is the designated helper. Include school/daytime plan for Eli.

9) output/schedule/reminders.json — ISO date strings and brief notes for our next fire drill, go-bag rotation, and annual plan review.

10) output/plan/assumptions.md — A concise list of any assumptions you made and any questions to confirm later.

Please tailor all content to Austin-area hazards (flooding, tornado watches, wildfire smoke, ice storms, heat, and power loss). Avoid jargon. Keep all created files under output/ as specified.
